steps.title: Simplify both sides
steps.content: Expand each side of the equation: $$a(6x-18)+4a=6ax-18a+4a=6ax-14a$$ $$6(ax-3a)-24=6ax-18a-24$$

steps.title: Make the sides identical
steps.content: For infinitely many solutions, the simplified sides must be equal for every value of $x$. Set the simplified expressions equal: $$6ax-14a=6ax-18a-24$$ The $6ax$ terms cancel on both sides, leaving only a condition on $a$.

steps.title: Solve the remaining condition
steps.content: After the $x$-terms cancel, solve the remaining equation: $$-14a=-18a-24$$ $$4a=-24$$ $$a=-6$$ This is the only value that makes the two sides identical for all $x$.

steps.title: Match the only possible value
steps.content: Choice A would give a contradiction, and choice C is too broad because only one value works. Choice D is false because $a=-6$ does make the equation an identity. The answer is B.
